paper_title: Photodegradation of Dyes Using Modified Diatomite

methods.description: This method describes the preparation of ferrihydrite modified diatomite (DMF) to improve its properties for photodegradation studies. The modification involves treating diatomite with NaOH and FeCl2 solutions, followed by oxidation and drying.

steps.description: DB samples (45 g) were immersed in 300 ml 6M NaOH solution at 90 °C for 2 h to partially dissolve Si.
steps.searchable_summary: Immerse diatomite samples in NaOH solution to dissolve silicon

steps.description: The mixture was immediately added to 300 ml of FeCl2.4H2O solutions of differing concentrations (1, 2, 3, and 4 M) and stirred and oxidized in air at room temperature (25 °C) for 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Add FeCl2 solution to diatomite mixture and oxidize

steps.description: The mixture was centrifuged for 30 min with the speed of 12000 rotations/min for assorted the separation.
The solid obtained through centrifugation was washed with distilled water and oxidized in air at room temperature overnight.
The mixture was centrifuged for 30 min with the speed of 15000 rotations/min for assorted the separation to remove excess NaOH.
The solid obtained through centrifugation was washed with distilled water and oxidized in air to continue the oxidation Fe(OH)2 and remove the excess of NaOH for 24h.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash and oxidize to remove NaOH and oxidize Fe(OH)2

steps.description: The mixture was dried at 105 °C for 24h.

methods.method_name: Photodegradation Study of Vat Green 03 Dye
methods.description: This method involves studying the photodegradation of Vat Green 03 dye using titanium dioxide and modified diatomite samples. The study aims to optimize the operating conditions for photodegradation.

steps.description: The pH of the Vat Green 03 solution was adjusted using sodium hydroxide (NaOH) or by hydrochloric acid (HCl).
steps.searchable_summary: Adjust pH of dye solution using NaOH or HCl

steps.description: The photodecolorization of Vat Green 03 dye textile using TiO2-DB, TiO2-DMF1, and TiO2-DMF4 were studied in pH range of 4-10.
steps.searchable_summary: Study photodecolorization of dye using TiO2 and diatomite

steps.description: The photocatalytic activity was determined by measuring the pH and the absorbance of samples every 15 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure pH and absorbance to determine photocatalytic activity
